title: Kaworus Appearance just Prior to 3I in EoE
display_title: Kaworus Appearance just Prior to 3I in EoE
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu's Appearance just Prior to 3I in EoE             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Mon Dec 02, 2013 2:09 pm This is something I've been wondering since I re-watched EoE the other day (for the millionth time). During the initiation of 3I, Shinji is freaking out while sitting in the cockpit of Eva Unit 01, which is floating in the sky. Then, he suddenly hears a voice say "Is this better?" He looks up to see a smiling Kaworu, and suddenly his panic completely dissipates as he conveys one of the happiest looking smiles he shows through the entirety of the series and asks "Have you been there the whole time?" This sense of happiness is what allows him to put down the walls and finally break down for 3I to initiate. Then he hears the voice of Yui say something like "This Rei is your heart. She embodies your hopes and your dreams". That line seems to mirror what the other Reis seem to be embodying when the convey everyone's "true loves". Such cases include: Hyuga seeing Misato, Fuyutski seeing Yui, and Maya seeing Ritsuko. Interestingly, this "seeing their true love" is used in a different manner. It also "breaks them down" but it literally does it by tearing down the emotions holding up their AT fields. Thus, they get liquified into pure LCL. Maya's case is the most interesting in that her level of despair at the point is similar to Shinji's and is almost instantly melted away (Just like Shinji) upon being embraced by Ritsuko. My question is this: Does Rei embody Shinji's true love to break him down by appearing as Kaworu? Some evidence for this idea: Maya's "true love" is the only that is of an apparent homosexual nature. The nature of her despair and the sense of awe (and the intensity of each) runs parallel to Shinji's. Thus, Anno might have directly made this parallel to tell us something deeper about Shinji: that the one he TRULY loves is Kaworu, not Asuka. Thus, like Maya, he too is conveying homosexual feelings by feeling the same sense of comfort that Maya did with "Ritsuko" by embracing "Kaworu". By implication, this also suggests that Kaworu wasn't actually there (at least when GNR was floating in the sky with UNIT 01) and that what Shinji saw was all GNR, i.e. Lilith fused exclusively with Rei via body and soul. Thus, Asuka has a legitimate reason to be angry that Shinji just might be using her as a comfort object during the kitchen/choking scene. What do you guys think about this?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 02, 2013 2:54 pm I'll just repost this from another thread . Reichu wrote: You seem to be suggesting (as it is often suggested) that GNK is the thing that appears before Shinji to Tang him the same way, say, Yui appears for Fuyu or Ritsuko appears for Maya. However, GNK merely subdues Shinji and Eva-01; he doesn't cause Shinji's reversion to LCL. GNR takes back the reins as Yui says, "This Rei is your heart", and Rei asks, "What do you desire?" as an image of a woman clutching her breasts appears. It's THEN that Shinji pops. Hence, we can infer that the Vision of Boobies (= That Which Is Desired) is what Shinji got instead of a Yui, Ritsuko, or Misato to guide him into Tang Land.

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Mon Dec 02, 2013 3:14 pm In regards to this: You seem to be suggesting (as it is often suggested) that GNK is the thing that appears before Shinji to Tang him the same way, say, Yui appears for Fuyu or Ritsuko appears for Maya. However, GNK merely subdues Shinji and Eva-01; he doesn't cause Shinji's reversion to LCL. GNR takes back the reins as Yui says, "This Rei is your heart", and Rei asks, "What do you desire?" as an image of a woman clutching her breasts appears. It's THEN that Shinji pops. Hence, we can infer that the Vision of Boobies (= That Which Is Desired) is what Shinji got instead of a Yui, Ritsuko, or Misato to guide him into Tang Land. I wasn't saying that GNR is not real, nor was I saying Kaworu wasn't resent LATER ON (you know, like AFTER Rei tells Shinji he's in the sea of LCL). I was just saying that GNR (who presumably has all the powers of the little Ries) used her shape shifting magic to take the form Kaworu in order to subdue him and break down his ego border. In a different way (but ultimately towards the same end- getting souls into the sea of LCL) she does the same thing to Maya, Ritsuko, Hyuga, and Fiyutski.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 02, 2013 3:30 pm Hmm, I may as well have not posted the quoted text at all... Kaworu appearing before Shinji is NOT what causes him to revert to LCL. We see Shinji surrender to bliss, but his body is still intact. Hence, the giant Kaworu is NOT Shinji's equivalent of Rei taking the form of Misato, Yui, Ritsuko, or whatever. Note that after Eva-01 fuses with the Spear of Longinus, the following sequence happens ( breakdown with pictures here ): - Rei returns to the front of GNR as Yui says, "This Rei is your heart, your hopes and dreams." - Rei asks, "What do you wish for?" - The easily-missed image of a naked Asuka-esque female figure clutching her breasts appears, as if in response to Rei's question. - Return to Shinji. His image starts to ripple, and eventually transitions to flowing water -- as if to suggest the transformation to LCL. The order of events and the principle of cause and effect suggests that it is the boobies that complete the destruction of Shinji's ego boundary and cause his reversion to LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Mon Dec 02, 2013 4:19 pm I kinda see your point. I never said "turned into LCL" I said break down his ego border. But anwyays, that's besides the point. However, the boobs are an indication of "mother" not mere a female. Hence, Shinji wants his mommy, not a sexualized woman. Which is interesting when you think about it, Shinji has two qualifying features that typically (but not always) surround the formation of a young male into a homosexual when they grow up: A domineering mother (through the Eva rather than in real life), and an absentee father. But also, what about the parallels between Maya's freak out/comfort and Shinji's? It's a known fact that Shinji is based off a female made into male. I think Maya fairs a bit like Shinji in resemblance. And she is drawn to a character of the same sex with light hair.... Another point I should make is that Asuka rejects Shinji in the Kitchen/Chocking scene on the grounds that he's merely using her as comfort object. To that end, he's trying to use her as a substitute (much like Rei is) for his mother. She's very assertive in that she doesn't want to be that (but instead wants to be a sexual female, and like a young girl crush suffers from unrequited love by dropping him the typical girl codes of "obvious/unobvious" hints). Shinji is hurt that she won't be a comforting mother for him and he's repulsed and disgusted by the sexual woman concept (just as he is when watching Kaji and Misato have sex in Misato's memories). He can't stand to see that side of womanhood. So, he violently reacts by choking her, just like Ritusko's mom choked out Rei I (a variation of the primary artificial mother of the series). He still has those same feelings on the beach. But his anger there is twofold: Asuka is not Kaworu (the one he REALLY wants) and she's still not being a mom to him. He only relents by when she decides "Okay, I'll give you what you want.." by caressing him the way Yui did before their departure. Then, he lets go and starts to cry because she's not his mommy and because Kaworu probably isn't coming back. Rei's presence and sudden disappearance in this scene helps to symbolize this: With her vanishing, the artifice of mother is finally gone and all that is left is something that is no use to him (hmm.. that kind of reflects "That old hag is no use" said by {a variation of the primary! artificial mother in Eva} Rei I to Gendo's motherly comfort object): a sexualized woman. Asuka sees how pathetic he is at that point and decides to kick him while he's down and makes the comment "How disgusting.." She's disgusted that he wants to be with another guy and that he would ever equate the concept of mother with the sexualized woman or get them twisted in any way. BOTTOM LINE: I think Kaworu's appearance during GNR's breakdown is meant to mirror the "true love" effect of what the little Reis do with the other characters. I think Shinji's true love is Kaworu. I think people desperately want to cling to the idea of Asuka and Shinji being eachother's "true loves" because of a hetro-centric mindset. I admit that it is hard to accept that Shinji is gay and that he Asuka won't ever be together. They simply aren't right for each other. She wants him as a true sexualized male and he wants her as a mother-like comfort object. Therefore the "romance" is one sided. EoE is about (above all else) acceptance. Asuka's "how disgusting" line is one of acknowledgement and acceptance, along the lines of the song Thanatos "We're not the same and it's making me sad. We can't ever be together" (or something like that). Like Kaji said "The Kanji we use for 'she' means 'a woman far away' and women will always be on a distant shore". I think that the beach scene is the epitome of this. Shinji's and Asuka's feeling and desires couldn't be further away from each other and their absolutely no reconciling the gap because there's no time left in the narrative itself at that point. Also, the TV ending could be seen as kind of "coming out of the closet" type scenario where Shinji learns to accept himself as gay and everyone congratulates him on it.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 02, 2013 4:33 pm SawItAtAge10 wrote: I kinda see your point. I never said "turned into LCL" I said break down his ego border.

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Mon Dec 02, 2013 4:35 pm What about the Maya connection?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Dec 02, 2013 4:49 pm Everyone assumes that the scene to which you refer suggests that Maya has sexual feelings towards Ritsuko, but note that the message Ritsuko types is not "I WANT YOU", "I DESIRE YOU", "I LOVE YOU", or any variation thereof but "I NEED YOU". Maya needs to be needed. Knowing that they're needed breaks down their barriers. It isn't even really true love that we're seeing in the tangification scenes- it's the fulfillment of a base desire. Hyuga transmigration is by contrast explicitly sexual, but is it romantic ? Would Eva really characterize Hyuga's sexual lust for an unattainable woman as true love ? The same question is easily asked of Fuyutsuki. Yui isn't even a woman to him, she's an ideal. His youth, maybe. "TRUE LOVE = TANG" is lacking in nuanced. It does not speak truth. It is desire that calls.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 02, 2013 4:55 pm SawItAtAge10 wrote: What about the Maya connection?

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Mon Dec 02, 2013 6:08 pm I will kind of admit that you got me on a lot of points. I'm not trying to explicitly say that Shinji and Kaworu are the perfect couple. "Creating new couple shippings, is not on my agenda, what blasphemy!" XD Seriously though, I don't really have an agenda. I'm merely trying to offer an alternative to what people always tend to say about Asuka and Shinji's feelings. Before I proceed, I should make clear that "true love" simply means the one that is desired most. With Maya,I certainly see the orgasmic thing, but I also see her smiling and reaching the apex of relieving happiness in that one moment. I see Shinji do the same thing when he sees GNK. I think the "romance" between Shinji and Asuka is like Helga and Arnold from Hey Arnold! (yes I went there...) Helga's always a bitch to Arnold and it gives him the false impression that she hates him. The same is true with Shinji. Asuka paradoxically says "I love you" by saying "What are you stupid?!" Shinji doesn't really say anything that would indicate that he wants her. The masturbation scene could be viewed as him desperately trying to reach her levels of sexuality, but by horrible synthetic means. He inevitably fails because masturbation is an empty substitute for actual love making, just as a mother or child is a poor substitute for a lover. Another interesting thing to note is that Kaworu is always seen in association with mothers (Yui) or artificial ones (Rei) he gives Shinji what was lacking: compassion (at least the feeling of it). This feeling came from one other person: Yui. So, in that sense, he's looking for that "motherly love" and comfort from all angles.... One other question: what about all of the connections between Asuka as a sexual woman/mother and the final disappearance of motherhood with ghost Rei in the beach scene? Based on how Shinji might feel rage towards Asuka for not fulfilling the duty as mother, what are your thoughts on these concepts?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Dec 02, 2013 6:19 pm Reichu wrote: There's some pretty erotic stuff going on. Ritsuko knees Maya in the crotch, and Maya's longing "SEMPAI! SEMPAI!" is punctuated with little hearts in the script.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 02, 2013 7:03 pm SawItAtAge10 : Funny you mention Kaworu's maternal associations; I think I'm one of the only people on this forum who's dared make such an observation in the past. It might be interesting to look more deeply into this sometime, and compare "Kaworu as Father" (the more overtly presented side, considering the Gendo comparisons) to "Kaworu as Mother" (the more sneakily presented side). Maybe not in this thread, though. I honestly don't think Asuka is supposed to represent a mother in any real respect (she even rejects the idea of being a mother one day). NGE shows Shinji surrounded by mother-figures on all sides (Yui/Eva, Misato, Rei) and clinging to maternal comforts, but, as a coming-of-age tale, he must grow up and come to terms with his independence and sexuality. Shinji's "mothers" are taken from him one after another: Misato's maternal nature is tainted by her sexuality and she leaves Shinji through her death; Rei turns out to be a serial clone of his mom and then a Japan-sized alien goddess, who ultimately dies and becomes a transcendent, silent observer; Yui leaves planet Earth to go on a voyage into the cosmos, and forever removing any future possibility of Shinji depending on Eva-01 for his sense of personal identity. At the end, Shinji is alone with Asuka, the female who refuses to be reduced to that infantile source of comfort, "Mother, the First Other". Instead, she represents an adult relationship (a "sexual woman", as you say), the Significant Other, and an equal. (In practical terms, however, Shinji and Asuka have way too many parental issues and various other traumata, along with generally being immature teenagers who still have a lot of growing up to do, for a mature adult relationship between them to be anything more than a distant pipe dream. But then, who ever said their relationship needed to be practically grounded to serve its narrative and symbolic purpose?) Chuckman wrote: Or ecstatic.

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Tue Dec 03, 2013 4:10 pm REICHU SAID: "Funny you mention Kaworu's maternal associations; I think I'm one of the only people on this forum who's dared make such an observation in the past. It might be interesting to look more deeply into this sometime, and compare "Kaworu as Father" (the more overtly presented side, considering the Gendo comparisons) to "Kaworu as Mother" (the more sneakily presented side). Maybe not in this thread, though." Here's another interesting consideration about Kaworu: he's a Christ-like figure. You might be thinking: wait, I thought Shinji was a Christ-like figure (I'll get to that in moment) Seeing Kaworu as Christ, we see Adam as being God. In that sense, we see an omnipotent being birthing itself and being incarnated into human form. This is precisely what God did when became Jesus in the Bible. This is taken further by the fact that Jesus is often interpreted as being the "second Adam", and because he is first "pure" sinless human before The Fall of Man and expulsion form the garden of Eden. Being an Angel, and his human reincarnation, Kaworu can very much be seen as the "second Adam" within the series... Then, of course there's his sacrifice, he tells Shinji that humans deserve to live to seize the future, and thus relinquishes his own eternal destiny of living forever. All these add up to being very much like Jesus in the Bible. Now, Shinji being Christ (or at least an artificially induced one) would cause Kaworu's sacrifice to mean something slightly different... If Shinji is Jesus, that would make Kaworu Judas who betrayed Jesus in the Bible. Kaworu betrayed Shinji's trust by being an Angel. Nevertheless, Kaworu is the true martyr here. In the context of the Bible, you can see Jesus' sacrifice at the Cross as being a false sacrifice because he knew that resurrection was part of the plan from the start. Therefore, he didn't actually lose or sacrifice anything. However, to get him to the cross, betrayal on Judas's part was necessary. The fucked up part that comes into play is that Judas traded away (or sacrificed) his eternal destiny by living up to being a tool towards Jesus' crucifixion. My point with this is thus: Judas is the true martyr of the story because his betrayal of Judas and subsequent eternal damnation for it were completely necessary for Jesus and God to pull off the plan of the New Testament. Likewise, manipulating Kaworu to his death was part of Nerv's plan to make Shinji into a kind of "neo-Christ" for the series. REICHU SAID: "I honestly don't think Asuka is supposed to represent a mother in any real respect (she even rejects the idea of being a mother one day). NGE shows Shinji surrounded by mother-figures on all sides (Yui/Eva, Misato, Rei) and clinging to maternal comforts, but, as a coming-of-age tale, he must grow up and come to terms with his independence and sexuality. Shinji's "mothers" are taken from him one after another: Misato's maternal nature is tainted by her sexuality and she leaves Shinji through her death; Rei turns out to be a serial clone of his mom and then a Japan-sized alien goddess, who ultimately dies and becomes a transcendent, silent observer; Yui leaves planet Earth to go on a voyage into the cosmos, and forever removing any future possibility of Shinji depending on Eva-01 for his sense of personal identity. At the end, Shinji is alone with Asuka, the female who refuses to be reduced to that infantile source of comfort, "Mother, the First Other". Instead, she represents an adult relationship (a "sexual woman", as you say), the Significant Other, and an equal." (In practical terms, however, Shinji and Asuka have way too many parental issues and various other trauma, along with generally being immature teenagers who still have a lot of growing up to do, for a mature adult relationship between them to be anything more than a distant pipe dream. But then, who ever said their relationship needed to be practically grounded to serve its narrative and symbolic purpose?) All of this points to my point about a non-sexual/non-romantic connection between Shinji and Asuka. Shinji masturbating over a comatose Asuka is hardly even romantic or grounded btw... Another thing to take note of is that "mother" in any sense is completely void in Asuka's first episode appearance Asuka Strikes! Rei isn't there, the first hints of Misato's more sexual overtones come about with Kaji's appearance, and Shinji taps into his more "girly side" by wearing Asuka's plug suit and synchronizing with her inside Unit 02. When Rei II dies, Misato tries to comfort Shinji through a slightly sexual act by touching him, he retracts and she wonders if he's afraid of all women. She had already had sex (as depicted at the end of episode 20) and the threshold of her "motherliness" is starting to fully dissipate. This is evidenced further by Pen Pen not wanting to come near here either. These are just more signs that Shinji is pulling away from desire for the "woman" in a sexual way. Then comes Kaworu in the absence of Asuka and takes her place in Unit 02 and ultimately controls by the end of the Episode 24. This could be an interpretation of replacement. Not only is Kaworu Unit 02's pilot replacement. He becomes Shinji's companionship replacement. Also, if one considers that in her truest form, and with her blatant rejection of motherhood, Asuka is a full representation of pure sexuality driven towards a female. In taking her seat, perhaps Kaworu alters the position to be seen as male sexuality and even sexuality driven towards males. One more thing, I think Maya's supreme joy is both one of pleasure and pain. It's kind of like the Ecstasy of St. Teresa statue. That image represents total fulfillment through a single act that brings pain (bring turned into LCL) and joy (embracing the one you desire most, feeling the sensations of an orgasm).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 05, 2013 7:19 pm Using quote tags tends to make things a bit more readable, just FYI.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 26, 2013 11:21 pm I agree with Reichu that a huge reason Shinji and Kaworu become so close so quickly is because Shinji had become emotionally isolated from everybody else in the show: Misato scared him away; Rei creeped the hell out of him; Asuka was comatose after her breakdown; and lastly, his classmates had all evacuated after 23. He was desperate for someone to care about him and nobody he already knew could fill the position; he was probably at his lowest point thusfar in the series, and then along comes Kaworu, who is literally too good to be true, offering a helping hand and being completely accepting of Shinji. This is completely uncharacteristic of Shinji's first impression of anybody in the show; nobody else that Shinji meets in the show is so completely sympathetic to Shinji upon meeting him. So, fast forward to EoE and GNR/GNK: Shinji is freaking the hell out over the appearance of a giant naked Rei slowly approaching him (as if the regular ol' Rei didn't already creep him out at this point). Misato was dead, and he knew it, and he felt incredibly guilt over what he had done to Asuka. Who else could calm him down other than Kaworu at this point? I disagree that the appearance of Giant Naked Kaworu is an extention of the Rei apparations. In Terminal Dogma, Rei fused with the Adam Embyro and then with Lilith; it's unclear where Kaworu's soul comes into play (it could have been just hanging around Terminal Dogma after death), but during the forbidden union Rei/Lilith becomes a singular being with Kaworu/Adam, and that's how Kaworu was able to appear to Shinji. I also disagree that the Rei apparations (AKA transition guides) are supposed to represent the target individual's "true love" in order to break down the barriers of the soul. They do represent intimacy between the target individual and the apparation, but not necessarily true love; maybe they're only there to comfort the target invidiual, in which case it just has to be someone close enough to the target individual (except for in Aoba's case). In that sense, they are acheiving the same purpose that Kaworu has for appearing in front of Shinji, but rememeber that GNR is unquestionably as much Kaworu as it is Rei because of their fusion. Lastly, I do agree that Kaworu has some easily overlooked maternal associations. Despite his male form that we are all familiar with, Kaworu is the reincarnation of Adam who is the progenitor of the Angels. He is the parallel opposite of Rei, the reincarnation of Lilith and the progenitor of the Lilin. Because of this paradigm, many associations with Rei can be subtly reassocated with Kaworu, especially that motherly aspect so frequently brought up with Rei. The way Kaworu loves Shinji is the same way a mother would unconditionally love her child; despite Shinji's flaws, Kaworu is supportive and encourages Shinji to grow and love himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 28, 2013 3:42 pm You can also overdo the freud, people - try to keep it grounded in what we actually see the characters do. The freud BS is not the purpose in itself; It exists as a framework to explain the characters and their relation to anything the viewer/society could learn from this chaos. Not every shred of kindness an acceptance is immediately "motherly", unless you want to subscribe to the fact that the characters couldn't possibly be worth of that on their own merits. But if you want to analyze it that way, it's probably less that Shinji has a problem with the "sexual side" of chicks in itself - he saw Asuka as a pontential lust object from the get go. Being awkward about that topic just amounts to being a teenager, possibly combined with being afraid or rejection and generally hesitant to openly show strong urges/feelings, but it's the same with anger and the like - he just keeps it bottled up untill it explodes because he didn't have this experience that you misbehave and your parents punish you, for good reason, but they won't stop loving you because of this. He spent his life trying to be as inoffensive as possible so he wouldn't be abandoned again, and now he's been through so much shit that he finally snapped, ruled that it weas all futile and told the earthball (including and represented by Asuka, who always embodied the "hardships of coexistence"/everything problematic in the world to him) to go fuck itself, so to speak. His problem here is people showing aspects that he didn't expect them to have/didn't fit the model of them he had built in his head, because he's incapable to 100% understand all of their inner workings, as for the pesky human condition/ as he rants himself in the train scene. "You hid this all from me!" He saw Misato as a mother figure, so he finds it a tad brain-bleach inducing/wouldn't have expected to see her screw someone and be all "animalistically released". He saw Rei as a gentle confidante ("Maiden archetype", perhaps), so hearing she's involved with all this crazy mad science stuff, and, last but not least, his mom, is gonna be disconcerting - he just thought of both of them in a very different way, but they're not defined archetypical fragments, they're whole people. He wasn't being malicious or entitled here, he was just using a categorization-happy human brain to try and understand them, and got scared/uncertain when they did things he couldn't predict. Because, with his insecurity and low self-esteem, any angle of unknown/unpredictable is just an opening through which betrayal and dislike towards him can seep in. He's young and inexperienced, so he doesn't get how much of this is caused by concepts of his own that could just easily be changed, and, for similar reasons hasn't understood that this is supposed to be bridged by trust. If anything, Anno's discussion of the "good/bad mother" concept in that june interview should be cited here, in the context about "accepting people as dynamic 3d things with both good and bad parts". Although speaking of "rejection" on Shinji's part is way too strong. He's simply not yet completed the process of becoming an adult, with the understanding that comes with that. Also, what would make an "interpersonal sexual attraction" with a man that different from one with a woman? Is there any less of a minefield with parent-related complexes and interpersonal chaos based on each other's feelings? It's not a slider with two opposite ends, you know? And that perception has caused a lot of hurtful things in history, allthough I know that none of these were in anyone's intention here. Is this supposed to be the opposite-sex version of "lesbians are just mad at men"? If Kaworu's percieved as more "comforting" than Asuka (if that is indeed what this scene wants to tell us), it probably has more to do with the way the two of them act (and how Shinji percieves it) than what kind of naughty bits they have. --- GNK as a transistion guide... hm. It doesn't matter if he's really one, he has the effect of one either way. Saying that it's not actually him when Adam is definitely present and he appears later on seems to require more assumptions than that it's simply Adam itself manifesting in a form influenced by his "last incarnation" It's not completely out of question that Shinji, being at the center of the process, would get the actual GNR to embody his transistion guide in parallel how the less directly involved ppl getting regular sides reis, tho. My ideas/interpretation of the transistion guide is that they were shown the person they want to see most at this point, perhaps someone who they care more about then themselves (so that seeing them overcmes the self-preservation instinct that keeps their AT-fields errect. Aoba had no such person, so they had to "force" him open. Ouch. ) or just someone who would calm them down, they all see people they admire or that they somehow percieve as superior - that would be true for Shinji and Kaworu as well, allthough Kaworu probably sees this very differently. Who they want to see or actually care about has no bearing on wether they'd actually fit together/are a "true love". If Maya saw Ritsuko, Ritsuko would probably see Gendo. --- Also, this, "Asuka as the one equal relationship" is pure bogus. If you say that "Shinji has been incapable of forming equal relationships because he longs for symbiotic systems where he's the submissive part" (regardless of wether that's completely or partially true), then it should be equally apparent that Asuka is just as incapable of forming equal relationships because she has a need of being the dominant part, because the mere possibility of submitting scares her/she doesn't want to end up like Kyoko, whom she percieved as someone who submits. Sure, she does have some longing for a friend she can interact with/do things on equal grounds and, at times even a "protector", because all humams do (just like Shinji is very capable of being pissed at someone and even wanting others to depend on him), but she tries her hardest to stomp that part of herself into the ground/repress it away. she can't stand equals, anyone who gets close to being equal to her becomes a threat, day and night she's concerned with making sure that she "is not the same as others!" If you stuck them together, they'd just naturally exascerbate each other's complexes. They'll find the other very easy to boss around/be bossed around by. That's why the plot forces them into each other's vincinity - because there will aways be people that just drive them crazy, and they'll have to learn to coexist with people - even people like those.

chunks.content:
Article: Kaworus Appearance just Prior to 3I in EoE
[]: Kaworu's Appearance just Prior to 3I in EoE             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Mon Dec 02, 2013 2:09 pm This is something I've been wondering since I re-watched EoE the other day (for the millionth time). During the initiation of 3I, Shinji is freaking out while sitting in the cockpit of Eva Unit 01, which is floating in the sky. Then, he suddenly hears a voice say "Is this better?" He looks up to see a smiling Kaworu, and suddenly his panic completely dissipates as he conveys one of the happiest looking smiles he shows through the entirety of the series and asks "Have you been there the whole time?" This sense of happiness is what allows him to put down the walls and finally break down for 3I to initiate. Then he hears the voice of Yui say something like "This Rei is your heart. She embodies your hopes and your dreams". That line seems to mirror what the other Reis seem to be embodying when the convey everyone's "true loves". My question is this: Does Rei embody Shinji's true love to break him down by appearing as Kaworu? Some evidence for this idea: Maya's "true love" is the only that is of an apparent homosexual nature. The nature of her despair and the sense of awe (and the intensity of each) runs parallel to Shinji's. Maybe not in this thread, though. I honestly don't think Asuka is supposed to represent a mother in any real respect (she even rejects the idea of being a mother one day). NGE shows Shinji surrounded by mother-figures on all sides (Yui/Eva, Misato, Rei) and clinging to maternal comforts, but, as a coming-of-age tale, he must grow up and come to terms with his independence and sexuality. Shinji's "mothers" are taken from him one after another: Misato's maternal nature is tainted by her sexuality and she leaves Shinji through her death; Rei turns out to be a serial clone of his mom and then a Japan-sized alien goddess, who ultimately dies and becomes a transcendent, silent observer; Yui leaves planet Earth to go on a voyage into the cosmos, and forever removing any future possibility of Shinji depending on Eva-01 for his sense of personal identity. At the end, Shinji is alone with Asuka, the female who refuses to be reduced to that infantile source of comfort, "Mother, the First Other". Instead, she represents an adult relationship (a "sexual woman", as you say), the Significant Other, and an equal. (In practical terms, however, Shinji and Asuka have way too many parental issues and various other traumata, along with generally being immature teenagers who still have a lot of growing up to do, for a mature adult relationship between them to be anything more than a distant pipe dream. But then, who ever said their relationship needed to be practically grounded to serve its narrative and symbolic purpose?) Chuckman wrote: Or ecstatic.

 Asuka, who always embodied the "hardships of coexistence"/everything problematic in the world to him) to go fuck itself, so to speak. "You hid this all from me!" He saw Misato as a mother figure, so he finds it a tad brain-bleach inducing/wouldn't have expected to see her screw someone and be all "animalistically released". He saw Rei as a gentle confidante ("Maiden archetype", perhaps), so hearing she's involved with all this crazy mad science stuff, and, last but not least, his mom, is gonna be disconcerting - he just thought of both of them in a very different way, but they're not defined archetypical fragments, they're whole people. He wasn't being malicious or entitled here, he was just using a categorization-happy human brain to try and understand them, and got scared/uncertain when they did things he couldn't predict. Because, with his insecurity and low self-esteem, any angle of unknown/unpredictable is just an opening through which betrayal and dislike towards him can seep in. He's young and inexperienced, so he doesn't get how much of this is caused by concepts of his own that could just easily be changed, and, for similar reasons hasn't understood that this is supposed to be bridged by trust. If anything, Anno's discussion of the "good/bad mother" concept in that june interview should be cited here, in the context about "accepting people as dynamic 3d things with both good and bad parts". Who they want to see or actually care about has no bearing on wether they'd actually fit together/are a "true love". If Maya saw Ritsuko, Ritsuko would probably see Gendo. --- Also, this, "Asuka as the one equal relationship" is pure bogus. Article: Kaworus Appearance just Prior to 3I in EoE
's the submissive part" (regardless of wether that's completely or partially true), then it should be equally apparent that Asuka is just as incapable of forming equal relationships because she has a need of being the dominant part, because the mere possibility of submitting scares her/she doesn't want to end up like Kyoko, whom she percieved as someone who submits. Sure, she does have some longing for a friend she can interact with/do things on equal grounds and, at times even a "protector", because all humams do (just like Shinji is very capable of being pissed at someone and even wanting others to depend on him), but she tries her hardest to stomp that part of herself into the ground/repress it away. she can't stand equals, anyone who gets close to being equal to her becomes a threat, day and night she's concerned with making sure that she "is not the same as others!" If you stuck them together, they'd just naturally exascerbate each other's complexes. They'll find the other very easy to boss around/be bossed around by. That's why the plot forces them into each other's vincinity - because there will aways be people that just drive them crazy, and they'll have to learn to coexist with people - even people like those.
